scope.summary: Rust block_on reporting now follows tokio Runtime builder chains through receiver-preserving configuration methods before build/unwrap exposes the runtime receiver.

capability_alignment.capability: receiver-preserving builder configuration provenance for future-drive scheduling attribution
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
The verifier reuses the existing runtime-boundary-model and future-drive obligations.
Only builder methods that preserve the tokio runtime Builder receiver are accepted in this slice.
Builder callback hooks remain closed until callback demand/effect obligations are modeled for that surface.
reused_capabilities: shared future-drive scheduling and future-settled value-channel obligations

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Builder must be qualified or imported from tokio::runtime, unshadowed, and every intermediate method must preserve the Builder receiver.
The chain may only pass through receiver-preserving configuration methods before build produces the runtime Result.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: let rt = Builder::new_*().<allowed config methods>.build().unwrap(); rt.block_on(...)
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The existing last-visible-local-assignment receiver proof must still connect the local binding to the proven runtime constructor.

corpus_pricing.note: This source-prevalence count includes builder configuration uses outside block_on receiver chains; verifier spot checks below measure the behavior-changing reporting delta.

assertions: start_paused and unhandled_panic chains report future-drive and future-settled obligations when the builder root is proven.
thread_keep_alive, global_queue_interval, event_interval, and disable_lifo_slot preserve the builder receiver through build/unwrap.
on_thread_start, on_thread_stop, on_thread_park, and on_thread_unpark stay closed.
thread_name_fn stays closed because the callback-producing form still needs callback demand/effect proof.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no proof-backed Rust builder-config block_on bridge rows; the gate is used for hard soundness and runtime comparison.

summary.primary_reason_note: The moved units also carry async-block/await or task obligations, so the primary scheduling subreason may remain async-await by rule order.

closed_surfaces.surface: Builder callback hooks such as on_thread_start(|| ...).build().unwrap().block_on(...)
closed_surfaces.reason: Callback timing and effect obligations are not yet modeled for builder hook closures.
The function-valued naming hook remains a callback surface, not a simple receiver-preserving scalar configuration method.

closed_surfaces.surface: struct field initialization such as rt: Builder::new_*().start_paused(...).build().unwrap() followed by self.rt.block_on(...)
closed_surfaces.reason: Constructor-assigned field lifecycle and non-self field identity proof remain separate capabilities.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and complete receiver/type identity contracts remain missing evidence.

next_followups: Price constructor-assigned runtime fields only after field lifecycle, mutation, and non-self receiver identity proof exist.
Model builder callback hooks as callback demand/effect surfaces before allowing them to preserve future-drive receiver identity.
Keep exact block_on/await convergence closed until scheduling and result-channel contracts become proof-backed.
